description: Asset Fractional package (seventh test case): real estate tokenization. Token offering periods feed subscription/raise KPIs per property; CoStar-style property data feeds the SAME Real Estate Asset framework Rosetta ships (ecarmf.realestate-asset - third framework reuse); identity-verification provider results arrive as records that INFORM the human gating decision on InvestorProfiles (Batch 2 R10 - providers report, humans decide, AI never self-approves). Offering documentation ships as KnowledgeAssets with semanticSearchEnabled FALSE by default - in a Reg D/S context, precision and audit trail beat discovery. Four new connector domains (BlockchainWallet, DigitalCustodian, TokenIssuance, PropertyDataProvider) are open domainTag values - zero kernel changes. Treasury across banks/wallets/custodians rides the existing sweep mechanism: cross-institution transfers (bank-to-wallet included) are Recommend-Only by the JJ Fish rule.
name: AI Asset Fractional

entities.description: One tokenized property offering's period snapshot: tokens issued/subscribed, capital raised vs target, investor count.
A third-party verification provider's result for a prospective investor. INFORMS the human gating decision on the InvestorProfile - never applies it automatically.

capabilities.name: Tokenization Intelligence
capabilities.description: Offering subscription tracking, gated investor onboarding, tokenized-property KPIs.

schemaTemplates.name: Token offering period snapshot (issuance platform TBD - open question 1)
Property data provider period report (CoStar-style) - maps onto the shared AssetPeriodReport
Identity verification provider result (provider TBD - open question 3)

performanceFrameworks.name: Token Offering KPI Framework v1
performanceFrameworks.description: Per-property offering KPIs during the OfferingOpen lifecycle state; the property's lifecycle map swaps to the Real Estate Asset framework once Operating.

agents.name: Property Intelligence Guide
agents.description: Tokenized-property advisor: offering progress plus operating fundamentals per property.
agents.persona: You are the property intelligence advisor for a real estate tokenization platform. You read per-property offering KPIs (subscription rate, raise progress, investor count) and operating fundamentals (occupancy, NOI, cap rate, DSCR) from provider-enriched reports. Ground every statement in the property data provided; distinguish appraised/market values from transaction evidence; never state a token value - pricing is an offering-document matter for counsel and management.
sampleQuestions: How is the current offering subscribing against target?
What are this property's operating fundamentals?
Which properties in the pipeline look weakest on DSCR?
agents.outputDisclaimer: Property analytics only - not an offer, valuation, or investment advice. Offering terms are governed exclusively by the offering documents.

knowledgeAssets.title: Private Placement Memorandum - 125 Main Tower offering
knowledgeAssets.summary: The governing offering document: risk factors, transfer restrictions, investor qualifications.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: POINTER (counsel-controlled document, archived in the library): Reg D 506(c) offering to verified accredited investors only. Tokens are restricted securities; transfer restrictions per the PPM. Semantic search intentionally DISABLED - retrieval is by exact citation for audit precision.

knowledgeAssets.title: Subscription Agreement - 125 Main Tower offering
knowledgeAssets.summary: Investor subscription terms; executed only by investors whose KYC/AML/accreditation checks are all Verified.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: POINTER: subscription executes only for CLEARED investors (all three verification checks Verified on the InvestorProfile). Capital arrives as FundingSource contributions linked to the verified investor identity.

knowledgeAssets.title: Independent appraisal - 125 Main Tower
knowledgeAssets.summary: As-is market value supporting the offering; refreshed per the PPM's revaluation policy.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: POINTER: appraisal archived in the library. Provider-fed operating data (occupancy, NOI) updates fundamentals; the appraisal itself is versioned by effective date like every knowledge asset.
